summary: Reverser now recovers Win64 caller stack argument slots for direct callsites, and that native capability was used on 0x14062B170 to map the formatter call at 0x14062B263. The pending-event URL builder passes a stack-local output buffer, the literal format %s/%s/%s/%s?userHash=%s, two register string arguments, and three stack string arguments into 0x1401001D0.
confidence: high for callsite, literal format, caller stack slot offsets, and string component order; medium-high for semantic labels of the dynamic string-like fields because exact runtime names still require server/harness correlation.

tooling_delta.reverser_capability: pe-callsite-registers can now recover requested caller stack slots via --stack-arg, including source instruction, Win64 argument index, register-copy source origins, immediates, memory-loads, and stack-local LEA destinations.
tooling_delta.why_it_matters: Formatters and constructors in the 947 client frequently pass arguments beyond RCX/RDX/R8/R9 through [RSP+0x20] and above. This makes those substitutions machine-readable instead of requiring Ghidra-only manual stack tracing.

arguments.native_recovery: register-copy from RDI; branch-local literal assignments are visible at 0x14062B1B3, 0x14062B1AA, and 0x14062B1A1

arguments.source: selected pointer from root+0x18 with heap fallback through [R9]
MOV [RSP+0x20], RDX after selecting [global+0x48] or its heap pointer
MOV [RSP+0x28], RCX after selecting entry+0x08 or its heap pointer
MOV [RSP+0x30], R8 after selecting root+0x30 or its heap pointer

open_nxt_implications: The pending event endpoint model is now concretely ordered: event path, root path component, global session path component, entry path component, then userHash.

OpenNXT 947 traces that see event telemetry should compare request paths against events/<root-component>/<global-component>/<entry-component>?userHash=<root-userHash>, not just against the three event selector literals.

The tool can now be reused on session/open, session/close, JS5 publisher, and constructor wrappers where the fifth and later arguments carry key material, request pointers, or formatted endpoint fragments.
next_targets: Run --stack-arg recovery against session/open and session/close builders at 0x14062A760 and 0x14062AC81 to replace remaining manual stack-slot notes.
Apply the same native callsite stack recovery to JS5 publisher wrapper calls where constructor arguments exceed four registers.
Extend branch-sensitive source summaries so selector-dependent literals such as events/click and events/dismissal are emitted as alternatives rather than only the nearest linear assignment.
